title: Characters in Fonts 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, and 7
text: Characters in Fonts 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, and 7 Uniface provides keyboard translation tables that map all characters to Fonts 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, and 7. The descriptions for these meta character sets provide the following information: Font n View—shows what each character looks like, if possible. Not all Unicode characters are supported in all fonts and browsers, so if the character does not display correctly, consult the Unicode documentation at Unicode Character Code Charts By Script or $index Description—formal name of each character Keyboard—character’s location on a standard keyboard Unicode—hexadecimal Unicode value, or ‘N/A’ if not available Hexidecimal—hexadecimal value of the character Decimal—decimal value of the character Compose—combination of characters required to enter the character with the compose character facility Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
